title: Gemma overview
summary: Official landing page confirming Gemma 4 availability, Gemma ecosystem links, and supported integrations.
why_it_matters: Authoritative source for model family and official runtime links.

title: Gemma 4 page
summary: Official Gemma 4 page describing model sizes, multimodal capabilities, efficiency positioning, and download paths.
why_it_matters: Confirms Gemma 4 is real and intended for local and workstation deployment.

title: Run Gemma with Hugging Face Transformers
summary: Official HF integration guide showing Gemma 4 inference with Transformers and example model id `google/gemma-4-E2B-it`.
why_it_matters: Primary source for the HF runtime path used by this repo.

title: Run Gemma using PyTorch
summary: Official PyTorch guide for running Gemma directly, including multimodal examples and references to the Gemma PyTorch implementation.
why_it_matters: Useful reference for lower-level PyTorch access and model-format expectations outside the Transformers path.

title: Run Gemma with Ollama
summary: Official Ollama integration guide listing Gemma 4 tags `e2b`, `e4b`, `26b`, and `31b` and showing `ollama pull gemma4`.
why_it_matters: Primary source for the local Ollama baseline setup.

title: Gemma 4 model card
summary: Official model card covering Gemma 4 architecture, context lengths, parameter counts, capabilities, and best-practice generation settings.
why_it_matters: Best source for choosing initial Gemma 4 variants and generation defaults.

title: Gemma Scope docs
summary: Official interpretability docs for Gemma Scope 2 focused on Gemma 3, with links to SAEs and transcoders.
why_it_matters: Important for the SAE direction, while also showing current official scope support is not yet centered on Gemma 4.

title: Extracting Latent Steering Vectors from Pretrained Language Models
summary: Foundational paper showing latent steering vectors can be extracted from frozen language models and used for controlled generation and style transfer.
why_it_matters: Establishes the steering-vector idea before ActAdd and later activation engineering work.

title: Steering Language Models With Activation Engineering
summary: ActAdd paper introducing optimization-free steering via activation differences from contrast prompts, with strong results on sentiment and detoxification.
why_it_matters: The cleanest first method to implement in this repo.

title: Representation Engineering: A Top-Down Approach to AI Transparency
summary: Broad RepE paper introducing LAT, reading vectors, contrast vectors, and LoRRA for monitoring and control of high-level behaviors.
why_it_matters: Defines the broader methodology this workspace is built around.

title: Steering Llama 2 via Contrastive Activation Addition
summary: CAA paper averaging residual differences across positive/negative behavior examples to obtain more stable steering vectors.
why_it_matters: Natural next step after single-pair ActAdd.

title: Spectral Editing of Activations for Large Language Model Alignment
summary: SEA paper proposing covariance-based spectral activation editing with strong truthfulness and bias results.
why_it_matters: Provides a stronger data-efficient baseline beyond naive difference vectors.

title: How to use and interpret activation patching
summary: Tutorial paper on activation patching, metrics, and interpretation pitfalls.
why_it_matters: Useful for debugging and validating where interventions matter.

title: Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction
summary: Paper showing that refusal behavior in many chat models can be strongly mediated by a one-dimensional residual-stream direction.
why_it_matters: High-value evidence that important safety behaviors can be activation-steered and should be evaluated carefully.

title: Gemma Scope: Open Sparse Autoencoders Everywhere All At Once on Gemma 2
summary: Open suite of SAEs across Gemma 2 layers and sublayers, released to support broader interpretability research.
why_it_matters: Important bridge from dense activation steering to SAE-guided feature steering.

title: Steering Knowledge Selection Behaviours in LLMs via SAE-Based Representation Engineering
summary: SpARE paper using pre-trained SAEs to steer contextual vs parametric knowledge selection under knowledge conflict.
why_it_matters: Best concrete example of SAE-based inference-time representation editing in the gathered sources.

title: Improving Steering Vectors by Targeting Sparse Autoencoder Features
summary: SAE-TS paper using SAEs to measure and refine steering vectors to reduce side effects while preserving coherence.
why_it_matters: Strong pointer for future upgrades to this repo once the basic Gemma 4 loop works.

title: Representation Engineering repository
summary: Official RepE codebase with rep-reading and rep-control pipelines and example frontiers.
why_it_matters: Useful reference implementation for later extensions.

title: SpARE repository
summary: Official code for SAE-based representation engineering under knowledge conflict.
why_it_matters: Useful implementation reference for future SAE experiments.

title: Gemmaverse Google variants collection
summary: Hugging Face collection page grouping Google-published Gemma-adjacent variants and related release collections, including Gemma Scope and ShieldGemma references.
why_it_matters: Useful index for future expansion from base Gemma steering into Scope, safety, embedding, and related Gemma-family work.
